title: (PDF) 2007 The Mystery of the Bayon Face Towers - Academia.edu
highlights:
examines the cultural and religious shifts in the Bàyon temple complex during and after the reign of Jayavarma Parameśvara, focusing on the iconography of the face towers. It questions the interpretations of the faces as Buddhist or Shaiva and discusses evidence of religious tolerance and the alterations made post-Jayavarman VII. title: Jayavarman VII: The Monarch Who Transformed Angkor Wat into a ...
highlights: Angkor Wat, originally dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu, underwent a profound transformation into a Buddhist  ...    Jayavarman VII ascended the throne in the late 12th century, following the devastating Cham invasion of Angkor. A devout Mahayana Buddhist, strongly influenced by his wife Indradevi, he saw Buddhist compassion and wisdom as guiding principles for both government and personal conduct. His reign marked a dramatic shift: Buddhism became the Khmer state religion for the first time. Jayavarman VII constructed new Buddhist monuments, notably Angkor Thom and the Bayon temple, and pointedly invested in hospitals, rest  ...    While Jayavarman VII poured resources into new Buddhist temples, his religious policies had ripple effects throughout the empire, including Angkor Wat. Originally a Hindu temple, Angkor Wat’s gradual conversion began in Jayavarman’s  ...
